mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates short-term positive market momentum during economic expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of rising INDPRO periods, or the signal reverses direction during recessionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 245 while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, real rates turn negative, prompting market repricing and negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the inflation-rate spread shows no predictive IC over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or payroll growth fails to correlate with equity performance when unemployment is stable.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 revisions, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising retail sales indicates supply chain bottlenecks and inventory depletion, triggering negative equity returns during inflationary periods.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines while RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 60% of divergence instances, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation remains below 2.0%.
